channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world

item.title: Lawmakers hold moment of silence for slain Omer Neutra as thousands mourn in hometown synagogue
item.description: A bipartisan group of lawmakers held a moment of silence for the slain Omer Neutra, an American-Israeli who was killed by Hamas on Oct. 7, 2023, according to new intelligence uncovered by the IDF.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: A major power plant fails in Cuba, plunging the island into darkness — again
item.description: HAVANA (AP) — A new, widespread power outage plunged Cuba into darkness on Wednesday after one of the island’s major power plants failed, leaving millions without electricity and forcing authorities to suspend classes and work activities indefinitely.
item.dc:creator: ANDREA RODRÍGUEZ, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump appoints former Middle East negotiator as hostage affairs envoy
item.description: President-elect Trump on Wednesday announced his selection of Adam Boehler to serve as special presidential envoy for hostage affairs in his second term. While the position holds the rank of an ambassador, it does not require Senate confirmation.
item.dc:creator: Laura Kelly, The Hill

item.title: Trump picks Monica Crowley for senior State Department post
item.description: President-elect Trump on Wednesday nominated Monica Crowley, a former Fox News contributor who faced public scrutiny during Trump’s first term over allegations of plagiarism, for a top public relations post in the State Department.
item.dc:creator: Laura Kelly, The Hill

item.title: South Korean President Yoon's martial law declaration raises questions over his political future
item.description: SEOUL, South Korea (AP) — President Yoon Suk Yeol’s stunning martial law declaration lasted just hours, but experts say it raised serious questions about his ability to govern for the remaining 2 1/2 years of his term and whether he will abide by democratic principles.
item.dc:creator: HYUNG-JIN KIM and KIM TONG-HYUNG, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Hamas issues instructions to 'neutralize' hostages if Israel attempts rescue operation
item.description: Hamas threatened to "neutralize" its hostages if Israel attempted another rescue operation, according to an internal statement seen by Reuters.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Hispanic senators press Biden to protect immigrants before leaving White House
item.description: The Senate’s three current Hispanic Democrats are calling on President Biden to extend and strengthen humanitarian protections for certain groups of immigrants whose legal status could be stripped by the incoming Trump administration. In a letter on Wednesday, Sens. Catherine Cortez Masto (Nev.
item.dc:creator: Rafael Bernal, The Hill

item.title: Romania's surprise front-runner says he's not pro-Russia. But Romania alleges he had Moscow's help
item.description: The surprise front-runner in Romania’s presidential election insisted to The Associated Press in an interview Wednesday that he is not pro-Russian — hours before Romania declassified intelligence alleging Moscow ran an online campaign to promote him to voters.
item.dc:creator: Stephen Mcgrath And Vadim Ghirda, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: NATO chief urges European allies to ramp up defense spending as Trump returns to White House
item.description: NATO Secretary-General Mark Rutte is leading a fresh push for European countries to ramp up defense spending.
item.dc:creator: Lorne Cook, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: South Korea opposition parties move to impeach president over martial law declaration
item.description: Opposition parties moved to impeach South Korean President Yoon Suk Yeol on Wednesday over his sudden declaration of martial law, which thrust one of the U.S.'s closest allies into peril.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, The Hill

item.title: Japanese court convicts Australian who says she was tricked into smuggling drugs
item.description: CHIBA, Japan (AP) — A Japanese court on Wednesday sentenced an Australian woman to six years in prison for smuggling amphetamines into the country, despite accepting her testimony that she was tricked as part of an online romance scam.
item.dc:creator: MARI YAMAGUCHI, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Syria's insurgency leader tours seized city of Aleppo, as fierce battles intensify near Hama
item.description: The leader of Syria’s most powerful insurgent group has toured the seized city of Aleppo in a surprise visit, the first since the insurgents captured large parts of the city over the weekend as fierce fighting intensifies in the government-led counter-offensive in northern Hama.
item.dc:creator: Kareem Chehayeb, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: French lawmakers vote to oust prime minister in the first successful no-confidence vote since 1962
item.description: France’s far-right and left-wing lawmakers have joined together to vote on a no-confidence motion prompted by budget disputes that forces Prime Minister Michel Barnier and his Cabinet to resign.
item.dc:creator: Tom Nouvian And Sylvie Corbet, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Syrian insurgents capture four central towns as government forces reclaim some territory
item.description: BEIRUT (AP) — Syrian insurgents captured four new towns early Tuesday, bringing them closer to the central city of Hama, opposition activists said, while government forces retook some territory they lost last week.
item.dc:creator: BASSEM MROUE, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Biden says 'Africa is the future' as he pledges millions more on the last day of Angola visit
item.description: President Joe Biden has pledged another $600 million for an ambitious multi-country rail project in Africa as one of the final foreign policy moves of his administration.
item.dc:creator: Will Weissert, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Demonstrators again converge on Georgia's parliament after the country's EU bid was suspended
item.description: TBILISI, Georgia (AP) — Thousands of demonstrators in the Georgian capital converged on parliament again on Tuesday, venting outrage against the governing party's decision to suspend negotiations on joining the European Union.
item.dc:creator: SOPHIKO MEGRELIDZE, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: South Korea lifts president's martial law decree after lawmakers reject military rule
item.description: SEOUL, South Korea (AP) — The president of South Korea early Wednesday lifted the martial law he imposed on the country hours earlier, bending to political pressure after a tense night in which troops surrounded parliament and lawmakers voted to reject military rule.
item.dc:creator: KIM TONG-HYUNG, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Middle East latest: Israeli strikes on Gaza hospital wound 3, Netanyahu vows 'iron fist' in Lebanon
item.description: Israeli drone strikes hit Kamal Adwan Hospital on Tuesday, wounding three medical staff at one of the few hospitals still partially operating in the northernmost part of Gaza, the facility’s director said. Dr. Hossam Abu Safiya said the drones were dropping bombs, spraying shrapnel at the hospital.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: South Korean president, parliament clash over martial law: 5 takeaways
item.description: South Korean President Yoon Suk Yeol declared emergency martial law Tuesday, accusing the parliament of being a “den for criminals” and of “attempting to overthrow” the nation’s democracy.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, The Hill

item.title: South Korean parliament blocks president's martial law declaration
item.description: South Korean President Yoon Suk Yeol attempted to declare martial law in the country for the first time in 45 years but was blocked by a parliament vote early Wednesday morning local time. He vowed to lift the order after hours of chaos and protests in the capital of Seoul.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, The Hill

item.title: Chicago welcomes eight children from Gaza for medical treatment
item.description: Over the next few months, the children will receive medical treatment at U.S. hospitals, free of cost.
item.dc:creator: Emmy Victor, NewsNation

item.title: US blasts countries that ‘stood in the way of progress to protect their profits’ over collapsed plastic talks
item.description: The White House criticized a group of oil-rich countries as well as corporations who make money from plastic production in a new statement after global talks for a treaty to reduce plastic waste collapsed.
item.dc:creator: Rachel Frazin, The Hill

item.title: Palestinians say Hamas, Fatah close to agreement on committee for postwar Gaza
item.description: CAIRO (AP) — Palestinian officials say Fatah and Hamas are closing in on an agreement to appoint a committee of politically independent technocrats to administer the Gaza Strip after the war. It would effectively end Hamas' rule and could help advance ceasefire talks with Israel.
item.dc:creator: SAMY MAGDY and JOSEF FEDERMAN, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: IDF soldiers accuse UN peacekeepers of enabling Hezbollah terrorists amid increasing cease-fire violations
item.description: In southern Lebanon, Israeli forces confront entrenched Hezbollah positions and question UNIFIL’s ability to enforce its mandate even before the cease-fire agreement was drawn up.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Guinea stadium stampede kills 56 following clashes at soccer match
item.description: Reports said security had tried to use tear gas to restore calm after the chaos that followed a disputed penalty call.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Hong Kong launches panda sculpture tour as the city hopes the bear craze boosts tourism
item.description: Thousands of giant panda sculptures will greet residents and tourists starting Saturday in Hong Kong, where enthusiasm for the bears has grown since two cubs were born in a local theme park.
item.dc:creator: Kanis Leung, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Volkswagen workers hold strikes to push back against proposed pay cuts
item.description: Volkswagen workers launched rolling strikes at nine plants across Germany to underscore their resistance to pay cuts
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Largest gold deposit in the world worth $83 billion found in China
item.description: A discovery of gold reserves, estimated to be worth $83 billion, was made in China last month. China is the world's largest gold producer, Reuters reports.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Canadian news publishers sue OpenAI over alleged copyright infringement
item.description: OTTAWA, Ontario (AP) — A coalition of Canadian news publishers, including The Canadian Press, Torstar, Globe and Mail, Postmedia and CBC/Radio-Canada, has filed a lawsuit against OpenAI for using news content to train its ChatGPT generative artificial intelligence system.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Middle East latest: 2 children and a woman crushed to death outside Gaza bakery amid food shortage
item.description: Two children and a woman were crushed to death Friday as a crowd of Palestinians pushed to get bread at a bakery in the Gaza Strip amid a worsening food crisis in the war-ravaged territory, medical officials said.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Customers at this Starbucks can sip coffee and observe a quiet North Korean village
item.description: Coffee drinkers can sip their beverages and view a quiet North Korean mountain village from a new Starbucks at a South Korean border observatory.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Powerful Mexican cartel accused of forced labor, human trafficking
item.description: The Gulf Cartel is a long-standing, powerful drug trafficking organization that operates throughout Tamaulipas.
item.dc:creator: Sean Noone, NewsNation
